comment: ce-tabnet model registry. The honest source of model dimensions + tab math. JSON so it can be fetched by the browser (orchestrate.html), imported by the DO planner, and read by tooling. Mirrors web/model-config.js MODELS but is the canonical data file; model-config.js may import this. Money/perf numbers are approximate and documented in docs/scaling-to-300b.md.

defaults.per_tab_weight_bytes_note: 1.0 GiB conservative WebGPU weight budget per tab. Phones ~0.5 GiB, good laptops ~2 GiB. The planner overrides this per-device from reported caps; this is the fallback used for 'tabs needed' display.

bytes_per_param_by_quant._note: Bytes per parameter INCLUDING quant block overhead. q4_0 = 4 bits data + scale per 32-elt block ~= 4.5 bits/param = 0.5625 B. Used to recompute approx_weight_bytes if params is known but a pack is not yet produced.

tinyllama-1.1b-q4.label: TinyLlama 1.1B (Q4) — the bundled demo model
tinyllama-1.1b-q4.notes: Fits in one tab; force it across 2-3 stages to exercise the pipeline. Verify correctness against an unsharded reference first (docs/scaling-to-300b.md §5).

qwen2.5-0.5b-q4.label: Qwen2.5 0.5B (Q4) — tiniest demo
qwen2.5-0.5b-q4.notes: Qwen-family is Llama-compatible for tabnet's layer layout. Large vocab => embed/lm_head are a sizeable fraction; assign them to a beefier tab.

llama-7b-q4.label: Llama 7B (Q4) — first genuinely useful sharded model
llama-7b-q4.notes: ~3.5 GB => 4 tabs @1GB, 2 tabs @2GB. Smallest model worth sharding for real.

llama-70b-q4.label: Llama 70B (Q4)
llama-70b-q4.notes: ~35 GB => ~35 tabs @1GB, ~18 @2GB. The honest 'cluster of tabs' begins here.

llama-405b-q4.label: Llama 3.1 405B (Q4) — the honest '300B-plus' aspirational target

tab_math.comment: 405B is the most credible open dense '300B-plus' target. 200 GB @ Q4 => the honest '~100-300 tabs' range. The pack must be produced (models/README.md) before this entry runs. The framework assigns this S with the SAME code path as the 3-tab demo.
tab_math.per_layer_note: per_layer_bytes (~1.57 GB) EXCEEDS a 0.5-1 GB phone budget. Such devices need sub-layer split objects (manifest 'parts': attn/mlp) — the planner emits split hints; see plan.js splitHints.

llama-405b-q4.notes: Aspirational. Dense => clean contiguous layer-range sharding. Stage 0 also holds embed (~1.18 GB @Q4 at 128k vocab x 16384); last stage holds norm + lm_head (~1.18 GB). Give those to the two beefiest tabs.

llama-300b-dense-q4.label: Hypothetical dense 300B (Q4) — the literal tagline number

tab_math.comment: The exact tagline arithmetic: 300B x 4 bits / 8 = 150 GB. dims are an illustrative dense config (no single open 300B dense model exists today; use llama-405b-q4 as the real target). Present so 'tabs needed' for the literal 300B number is computable.

llama-300b-dense-q4.notes: HYPOTHETICAL config for the literal 300B math. Do NOT claim a real 300B run from this entry — it is dimensioning only. The producible target is llama-405b-q4.
